ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the movie in the catalog */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the movie was released */,
  "runtime_minutes" INTEGER /* Runtime of the movie in minutes */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average rating of the movie */,
  "votes" INTEGER /* Number of votes received by the movie */,
  "revenue_millions" REAL /* Total revenue generated by the movie in millions of dollars */,
  "metascore" INTEGER /* Metascore of the movie */,
  "budget_millions" REAL /* Budget of the movie in millions of dollars */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* Production company of the movie */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the movie */,
  "imdb_id" TEXT /* IMDB ID of the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre entry */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie the genre is associated with */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_genres_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Name of the director */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biography of the director */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the director */,
  "death_date" TEXT /* Death date of the director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Name of the actor */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biography of the actor */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the actor */,
  "death_date" TEXT /* Death date of the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_directors" (
  "movie_director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie-director relationship */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* ID of the director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_director_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_directors_director_id FOREIGN KEY ("director_id") REFERENCES directors ("director_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_directors_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_actors" (
  "movie_actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie-actor relationship */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character played by the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_actors_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_actors_actor_id FOREIGN KEY ("actor_id") REFERENCES actors ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (admin, analyst, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., movie ratings, revenue, etc.) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_contents" (
  "report_content_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report content */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_content_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_contents_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)
column_names: Rank of the movie in the catalog
Runtime of the movie in minutes
Number of votes received by the movie
Total revenue generated by the movie in millions of dollars
Budget of the movie in millions of dollars
Unique identifier for each genre entry
ID of the movie the genre is associated with
Unique identifier for each movie-director relationship
Unique identifier for each movie-actor relationship
Name of the character played by the actor
Role of the user (admin, analyst, etc.)
ID of the user who generated the report
Type of the report (e.g., movie ratings, revenue, etc.)
Unique identifier for each report content
